title: [skip-ci] RPM: Add riscv64 to ExclusiveArch-es
body:
podman-5.4.1 as found in rawhide , builds and seems to work without issues on currently available riscv64 machines

comments.body:
> 1. Did podman-machine subpackage work for you on riscv64 ? FWIW, I don't think anyone here is testing on riscv64 yet.

Sorry, that was just mechanical change without actually testing machines. I've removed that change.

> 2. Do you have links to podman scratch builds on riscv64?

There are few pre 5.4.1 non-scratch builds in the RISC-V koji  http://fedora.riscv.rocks/koji/packageinfo?packageID=19999
The build there works because riscv64 in the golang_arches_future  rpm macro (itself inherited from go-srpm-macros)
I think podman has been working without changes without issues for a while.
I just mock built locally 5.4.1 without any changes and it also seems to work.
The riscv64 is currently not supported in RHEL so it shouldn't break anything there. @Luap99 PTAL if podman-machine makes sense on riscv64.
